name: length_increases_after_set
code:
proof fn phi_1_length_increases_after_set<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(pre: Seq<K>, post: Seq<K>, i: int, k: K)
    requires
        sorted(pre),
        pre.no_duplicates(),
        0 <= i < pre.len(),
        i > 0 ==> pre[i - 1].cmp_spec(k).lt(),
        i < pre.len() - 1 ==> k.cmp_spec(pre[i + 1]).lt(),
        sorted(post),
        post.no_duplicates(),
        post == pre.update(i, k),
    ensures
        post.len() > pre.len(),
{
}
reason: set should preserve length; if the spec entails a length increase the ensures is wrong

name: frame_violation_other_index
code:
proof fn phi_2_frame_violation_other_index<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(pre: Seq<K>, post: Seq<K>, i: int, j: int, k: K)
    requires
        sorted(pre),
        pre.no_duplicates(),
        0 <= i < pre.len(),
        i > 0 ==> pre[i - 1].cmp_spec(k).lt(),
        i < pre.len() - 1 ==> k.cmp_spec(pre[i + 1]).lt(),
        sorted(post),
        post.no_duplicates(),
        post == pre.update(i, k),
        0 <= j < pre.len(),
        j != i,
    ensures
        post[j] != pre[j],
{
}
reason: Elements at untouched indices must be preserved; a frame violation means the update spec is too weak

name: target_element_not_k
code:
proof fn phi_3_target_element_not_k<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(pre: Seq<K>, post: Seq<K>, i: int, k: K)
    requires
        sorted(pre),
        pre.no_duplicates(),
        0 <= i < pre.len(),
        i > 0 ==> pre[i - 1].cmp_spec(k).lt(),
        i < pre.len() - 1 ==> k.cmp_spec(pre[i + 1]).lt(),
        sorted(post),
        post.no_duplicates(),
        post == pre.update(i, k),
    ensures
        post[i] != k,
{
}
reason: After set the element at index i must equal k; if the spec allows otherwise the update semantics are broken

name: single_element_vacuous
code:
proof fn phi_4_single_element_vacuous<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(pre: Seq<K>, post: Seq<K>, k: K)
    requires
        sorted(pre),
        pre.no_duplicates(),
        pre.len() == 1,
        sorted(post),
        post.no_duplicates(),
        post == pre.update(0int, k),
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: A single-element vec is a valid boundary case for set at index 0; if the spec is vacuously satisfiable here the preconditions are inconsistent

name: sortedness_broken_in_result
code:
proof fn phi_5_sortedness_broken_in_result<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(pre: Seq<K>, post: Seq<K>, i: int, k: K)
    requires
        sorted(pre),
        pre.no_duplicates(),
        pre.len() >= 2,
        0 <= i < pre.len(),
        i > 0 ==> pre[i - 1].cmp_spec(k).lt(),
        i < pre.len() - 1 ==> k.cmp_spec(pre[i + 1]).lt(),
        sorted(post),
        post.no_duplicates(),
        post == pre.update(i, k),
    ensures
        exists |m: int, n: int| 0 <= m < n < post.len() && !post[m].cmp_spec(post[n]).lt(),
{
}
reason: The post-state must be strictly sorted; if the spec entails the existence of an unsorted pair then valid() is not truly maintained
